task_completion.description: Load the reference roller coaster image into GeoGebra.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image 'coaster_loop.jpg' is visible in the GeoGebra graphics view.

task_completion.description: Reconstruct the loop's geometry as a clothoid using Fresnel integrals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra view shows a parametric curve defined using cos(t^2) and sin(t^2) (or similar Fresnel integral representations), and the curve is drawn in the Graphics view.

task_completion.description: Calibrate the scale based on a track gauge of 1.2 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A segment, measurement tool, or coordinate scaling is visible across the width of the track (the gauge) and is set to represent 1.2 units.

task_completion.description: Fit the generated clothoid curve to the track center line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The parametric clothoid curve visually aligns with the center line of the roller coaster loop in the background image.

task_completion.description: Identify the loop's height in meters with a point and label at the top.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct point is placed at the apex (top) of the loop, accompanied by a label displaying its height in meters (expected to be between 15 and 60).

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'coaster_reconstruction.ggb' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra window title shows 'coaster_reconstruction.ggb', or a save dialog confirms the file is being saved to '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/' with this name.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GeoGebra application open with active interaction in the Algebra and Graphics views, rather than terminal commands generating a .ggb file blindly.

integrity.description: The agent actually used the image to fit the curve.
integrity.visual_evidence: The reference image is visible in the background of the GeoGebra workspace behind the constructed curve, proving the curve was fitted to the specific photo.

integrity.description: The height label is derived from actual geometric construction, not just a spoofed text box.
integrity.visual_evidence: The height label is associated with a geometric point's properties or coordinates in the Algebra view, rather than just an isolated text annotation containing a hardcoded number.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected filename is 'coaster_reconstruction.ggb'. The reference image is located at '~/Documents/GeoGebra/assets/coaster_loop.jpg'. The track gauge used for scaling must be 1.2 meters. The calculated height of the loop should be between 15.0 and 60.0 meters (the real-world coaster, Revolution, is 27.43m tall).
